assertions.text: V1 was traveling east on a one-way exit ramp.
V2 (tractor-trailer) was parked on the paved shoulder of the exit ramp facing east.
V1 departed the roadway to the left, then returned to the roadway, then departed the roadway to the right before contact with V2.
V1's front plane contacted the back plane of V2's trailer (rear-end impact on V2).
There was a single distinct impact between V1 and V2.
At impact, V2 was pushed forward from its parked position and came to rest near the point of impact.
After impact, V1 rotated clockwise and bounced back, coming to rest on the left shoulder facing east.
The collision was forceful enough to push V2 forward and cause V1 to rotate and bounce back (moderate to significant impact).

verdicts.evidence: V1 was traveling at highway speed just before impact: [first_value 98.0 km/h at t=-4.75 s](T2) and [last_value 96.0 km/h at t=0.0 s](T2), consistent with travel along an exit ramp heading (direction not in sensors).
Telemetry contains no sensors for other vehicles' parked status or heading; the sensors only record V1. The presence and orientation of V2 on the shoulder is [unverifiable](T1).

Lane departures and sequence (left departure, return, right departure) are not directly measurable from V1 device-frame telemetry without position/GPS tracks; steering shows a peak at [40.5 deg at t=0.0 s](T2) but that does not confirm the described sequence.

Impact geometry (front plane of V1 to rear plane of V2) requires relative position/orientation or multi-vehicle sensors; crash-pulse is longitudinal with [final delta-V -98.2 km/h](T2) but axis alignment to vehicle front/rear is undocumented, so directionality is [unverifiable](T1).
A single CISS crash-pulse is recorded with a single peak delta-V at [t=0.24 s magnitude -98.6 km/h](T3) and final cumulative delta-V [ -98.2 km/h](T2), indicating one distinct impact event.
Telemetry does not record V2 motion; there is no sensor evidence that V2 was pushed forward or its resting point—this is [unverifiable](T1).

V1 rotation and final resting orientation on the left shoulder facing east are not directly provided: gyroscope/net-rotation data are not present in the available channels, so rotation direction and final pose are [unverifiable](T1).

The crash pulse shows a very large longitudinal delta-V with a peak cumulative change of [ -98.6 km/h at t=0.24 s](T3) and a final cumulative delta-V of [ -98.2 km/h](T2), which indicates a forceful impact consistent with pushing another vehicle and causing rotation/bounce.

result.rationale: Telemetry from V1 provides strong evidence of a single, very large longitudinal impact (delta-V ~[ -98.6 km/h](T3)/[ -98.2 km/h](T2)) and pre-crash speeds (~[98.0 km/h](T2) to [96.0 km/h](T2)), but lacks multi-vehicle relative position, GPS trace, or gyroscope orientation to verify vehicle-to-vehicle geometry, sequence of lane departures, or motion of V2.

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

detections.criterion: crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|
